notes_md:
**chịu khó** = hard-working, willing to put in effort. It’s about being patient and putting in steady work, even if it’s tiring or boring.

It describes a person’s attitude, not just skill. You can also use it for kids, students, workers—anyone who keeps trying instead of giving up.


Close meaning: **chăm chỉ** (hard-working). Opposite vibe: **lười** (lazy).

content.sentence: Bạn cần ___ hơn trong công việc.

translation.en: You need to work harder in your job.

exampleSentences: Em luôn chịu khó làm bài tập mỗi ngày sau giờ học.
Anh ấy rất chịu khó giúp đỡ đồng nghiệp trong công việc.
Chị chịu khó học tiếng Việt nên tiến bộ rất nhanh.

translation.en: You need to work harder in your job.

content.sentence: Bạn cần ___ hơn trong công việc.

exampleSentencesTranslated: You are always hard-working on your homework every day after class.
He is very hard-working in helping his colleagues at work.
She is hard-working in learning Vietnamese, so she improves very quickly.

definitions.vi: Là người siêng năng hoặc chăm chỉ.

example.en: She always works hard on her studies every day.
example.es: Ella siempre trabaja duro en sus estudios todos los días.
example.vi: Cô ấy luôn chịu khó học bài mỗi ngày.

notes:
chịu khó = hard-working, willing to put in effort. It’s about being patient and putting in steady work, even if it’s tiring or boring.

It describes a person’s attitude, not just skill. You can also use it for kids, students, workers—anyone who keeps trying instead of giving up.

Close meaning: chăm chỉ (hard-working). Opposite vibe: lười (lazy).
